purpose: production-shaped topology proof for one remote source, one local edited site, one drift witness, and one runner in Docker and Playground

remote_changed.role: same remote source site later in time

proof: remote-base seeds the persisted pull base
local-edited is the imported local site with user edits
remote-changed is the same remote identity observed later after drift
remote snapshot hash listing stays planning-only
dry-run and apply remain separate remote operations
apply revalidates fresh live evidence before every batch and at the storage boundary
journal inspect stays read-only
recovery starts with inspect before any mutating repair
authentication is at least as strict as current Reprint HMAC usage
runner owns preflight, remote snapshot hash listing, dry-run upload, apply, journal inspect, and recovery
remote-base and remote-changed are the same remote identity observed at different times
local-edited is the imported local site that produces the candidate plan
remote-base and remote-changed are the same remote identity at different times
browser-visible inspection uses the sandbox-provided 8080 ingress through a local-only proxy
runner is the only process allowed to preflight, list hashes, upload, inspect, revalidate, and recover
dry-run is a receipt, not a lock
journal inspection is read-only and never authorizes mutation by itself
recovery must begin with inspect before any mutating repair
remote-base and remote-changed are the same remote identity at different times
push preflight, dry-run, apply, journal, and recovery use the same route names as Docker
browser-visible inspection uses the sandbox-provided 8080 ingress through a local-only proxy
dry-run is a receipt, not a lock
journal inspection is read-only and never authorizes mutation by itself
recovery must begin with inspect before any mutating repair

auth_floor.required: at least as strict as current Reprint HMAC usage

test_topology.shape: one remote source site, one imported local site, and one later drift observation of the same remote identity

proof: remote-base seeds the persisted pull base
local-edited is the imported local site with user edits
remote-changed is the same remote identity observed later after drift
push preflight binds the persisted pull base to the live remote identity before any planning call
dry-run and apply remain separate remote operations
apply revalidates fresh live evidence before every batch and at the storage boundary
journal inspection is read-only and never authorizes mutation by itself
recovery must begin with inspect before any mutating repair
browser-visible inspection uses the sandbox-provided 8080 ingress through a local-only proxy
remote-base seeds the persisted pull base
local-edited is the imported local site with user edits
remote-changed is the same remote identity observed later after drift
push preflight binds the persisted pull base to the live remote identity before any planning call
push preflight, dry-run, apply, journal, and recovery use the same route names as Docker
journal inspection is read-only and never authorizes mutation by itself
recovery must begin with inspect before any mutating repair
browser-visible inspection uses the sandbox-provided 8080 ingress through a local-only proxy

pull_to_push_mapping.exporter: scans the merge base and coverage evidence
pull_to_push_mapping.importer: persists the base package as immutable provenance
pull_to_push_mapping.preflight: binds that persisted base package to the live remote identity and a short-lived session
pull_to_push_mapping.snapshot_hashes: lists the live remote comparison set for planning only
pull_to_push_mapping.dry_run: uploads the canonical plan as eligibility evidence and returns a receipt, not a lock
pull_to_push_mapping.apply: revalidates fresh live evidence before every batch and again at the storage boundary
pull_to_push_mapping.journal: reads durable evidence without authorizing mutation
pull_to_push_mapping.recovery_inspect: starts with inspect and classifies finish, rollback, retry, or block without mutation

required_invariants: dry-run and apply are separate remote operations
authentication must be at least as strict as current Reprint HMAC usage
journal inspection is read-only and never authorizes mutation by itself
recovery must begin with inspect before any mutating repair
browser-visible inspection stays on the sandbox-provided 8080 ingress through a local-only proxy
remote-base and remote-changed are the same remote identity observed at different times
